triage.explanation: Fast route by default; no hard factual risk trigger was found.

problem: Existing YOLO demand evidence dispatch must handle minor Claude JSON key drift and fail closed with explicit blockers on invalid agent JSON.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Minor recoverable JSON drift is recorded, unrecoverable JSON receives EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON, and PRD readiness stays blocked.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Minor recoverable JSON drift is recorded, unrecoverable JSON receives EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON, and PRD readiness stays blocked.
risks: Malformed provider output could be silently accepted or produce unclear blockers that hide readiness failure.

verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements minor JSON drift repair with parseJsonCandidate and fails closed with EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON error code when JSON cannot be recovered.

evidence.summary: parseJsonCandidate attempts direct JSON.parse, then applies four repair patterns on failure: line range stringification, backslash escaping, double-quote key normalization, and trailing comma removal. Returns {parsed, repaired: boolean, error: string}.
evidence.why: This is the core repair logic for recoverable Claude JSON key drift.

evidence.summary: extractJsonObject tries three extraction strategies in sequence: direct parse, fenced code block extraction (```json), and slicing from first { to last }. Each attempt invokes parseJsonCandidate with repair enabled.
evidence.why: This ensures JSON is extracted regardless of Claude formatting, with repair attempted at each stage.

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult checks if parsing succeeded. If !parsed, sets errorCode to EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON, returns a blocked result with the error code in result.error_code, and records parseError in the missing array.
evidence.why: This is the fail-closed behavior that blocks PRD readiness on unrecoverable JSON.
This conditional distinguishes JSON parse failures from provider runtime failures.

evidence.summary: Test repairs minor Claude JSON key drift simulates line range as 42-88 instead of string, escaped backslashes, and double-quote key drift. Asserts result.status is pass and result.provider_runs has json_repaired === true.
evidence.why: This proves that repair logic works and does not block dispatch when drift is recoverable.

evidence.summary: Test invalid agent JSON fails closed with explicit blocker code provides stdout: 'not json'. Asserts result.status is blocked, agent_results have error_code EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON, and readiness.blockers includes code EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON.
evidence.why: This proves that unrecoverable JSON blocks dispatch and PRD readiness with explicit error code.

evidence.summary: const parsedOutput = extractJsonObject(providerRun.stdout); const agentResult = normalizeAgentResult({action, providerRun, parsed: parsedOutput.parsed, parseError: parsedOutput.error});
evidence.why: This is the integration point where provider stdout is parsed, repaired if possible, and normalized into agent results.

evidence.summary: json_repaired: parsedOutput.repaired === true is recorded in provider_runs array
evidence.why: This provides an audit trail of which agent outputs required JSON repair.

evidence.summary: readiness = inspectDemandPrdReadiness is called with evidence_results. If boundary violations occur, blockers are added and prd_ready becomes false. Final result.status and result.code depend on readiness.prd_ready.
evidence.why: This shows how agent errors propagate to PRD readiness blocking.

risks: If parseJsonCandidate repair patterns do not cover new Claude drift variants, valid JSON could be incorrectly marked as EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON.
If extractJsonObject slicing strategy matches too broadly, it could extract partial JSON that passes parse but loses context.

result.notes: Evidence confirms implementation matches demand: minor drift is repaired via parseJsonCandidate with four regex patterns, unrecoverable JSON receives EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON error code, and PRD readiness stays blocked. Test coverage validates both repair and fail-closed paths.

agent_results.claim: Verified that explorer evidence is accurate: all line numbers, summaries, and code references match actual source files. The claim that YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements minor JSON drift repair with parseJsonCandidate and fails closed with EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON is supported by traceable project-scoped evidence.

evidence.summary: parseJsonCandidate implementation verified: direct JSON.parse attempt followed by four repair patterns (line range stringification, backslash escaping, double-quote key normalization, trailing comma removal). Returns {parsed, repaired: boolean, error: string}. Explorer summary matches actual code.
evidence.why: Core repair logic verification confirms the first part of the claim.

evidence.summary: extractJsonObject implementation verified: three sequential extraction strategies (direct parse at line 351, fenced code block at lines 354-358, slicing strategy at lines 360-365). Each strategy calls parseJsonCandidate with repair enabled. Explorer summary matches actual code.
evidence.why: Extraction strategy verification confirms JSON is parsed with repair regardless of Claude formatting.

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult implementation verified: line 371 checks if !providerRun.success || !parsed || typeof parsed !== 'object'. Line 372 sets errorCode to 'EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON' when !parsed. Lines 373-396 return blocked result with error_code in result field. Line 385 records parseError in missing array. Explorer summary matches actual code.
evidence.why: Fail-closed behavior verification confirms PRD readiness blocking on unrecoverable JSON.

evidence.summary: Test repairs minor Claude JSON key drift verified: line 551 injects line range drift, line 552 injects backslash drift, line 553 injects double-quote key drift. Line 566 asserts result.status is 'pass'. Line 567 asserts json_repaired === true. Explorer summary matches actual test.
evidence.why: Test coverage verification confirms repair logic works and does not block dispatch.

evidence.summary: Test invalid agent JSON fails closed with explicit blocker code verified: line 591 provides stdout 'not json'. Line 597 asserts result.status is 'blocked'. Line 598 asserts error_code is 'EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON'. Line 599 asserts readiness.blockers includes the error code. Explorer summary matches actual test.
evidence.why: Test coverage verification confirms unrecoverable JSON blocks dispatch and PRD readiness.

evidence.summary: Integration with PRD readiness verified: line 575 calls inspectDemandPrdReadiness with evidence_results. Lines 585-596 add boundary blockers if violations detected. Lines 598-600 set result.status and result.code based on readiness.prd_ready. Explorer summary matches actual code.
evidence.why: Integration point verification confirms agent errors propagate to PRD readiness blocking.

risks: Explorer-identified risk stands: if parseJsonCandidate repair patterns do not cover new Claude drift variants, valid JSON could be incorrectly marked as EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON.
Explorer-identified risk stands: if extractJsonObject slicing strategy matches too broadly, it could extract partial JSON that passes parse but loses context.

result.notes: All explorer evidence verified against actual source files and tests. Line numbers are accurate, summaries match implementation, and the claim is fully supported by project-scoped evidence. No assumptions presented as facts, no missing data, and risks are properly identified.
